ei.headline: Market Expansion Alliance
ei.context_note: Ei day: auspicious for career advancement
ei.detail: The partnership can rapidly open new markets. Co-branding events, joint product launches, strategic investments — all yield above-average returns right now. Good timing to sign framework agreements.
do: Launch co-branding activities or joint product releases
Propose strategic investments or joint venture deals
Build joint market expansion plans
Pursue joint case studies with the partner's flagship clients
avoid: Missing the expansion window with small-scale partnerships
Being overly conservative on profit sharing

shin.headline: Deep Commitment Alliance
shin.context_note: Shin day: auspicious for forming bonds
shin.detail: The other party is willing to invest resources in building a long-term relationship. Ideal for negotiating exclusivity, long-term supply contracts, and technology licensing.
do: Propose a long-term strategic partnership framework
Negotiate technology licensing or IP sharing agreements
Establish regular executive dialogue mechanisms
Co-invest in shared R&D infrastructure
avoid: Taking short-term profits and exiting
Rejecting the other party's investment goodwill

yu.headline: Ecosystem Building Alliance
yu.detail: Find partnership opportunities through industry networking and ecosystem cultivation. Joint trade shows, industry forums, and ecosystem events surface natural partners.
do: Co-attend industry trade shows and forums
Build an ecosystem partner network
Co-host industry events for visibility
Build trust through mutual customers
avoid: Networking without setting partnership boundaries
Treating every relationship as a deep partnership

sui.headline: Internal Consolidation Period
sui.context_note: Sui day: suitable for recovery only
sui.detail: Not the right time to initiate new external partnerships. Focus on auditing existing partner relationships — review contract fairness, trim unprofitable alliances, renegotiate imbalanced terms.
do: Audit existing partners' input-output ratios
Trim partnerships that don't deliver value
Renegotiate imbalanced contract terms
Compile partnership performance data for internal review
avoid: Launching new strategic alliances
Signing new long-term contracts
Committing new resources in negotiations

an.headline: Foundation Building Alliance
an.context_note: An day: auspicious for relocation and construction
an.detail: Good timing to build partnership infrastructure. Sign foundational agreements, deploy shared platforms, set up joint offices, implement collaboration systems. Solid foundations make everything after easier.
do: Build partnership SOPs and process frameworks
Deploy shared technology platforms
Set up joint project management mechanisms
Partner with companies expanding into new markets
avoid: Rushing for results while skipping infrastructure
Scaling the partnership before processes mature

kai.headline: Disruption Alliance
kai.context_note: Kai day: only for overcoming obstacles
kai.detail: Good timing to resolve tough partnership problems: clear disputed contracts, handle breach situations, terminate unhealthy partnerships. Destructive but necessary actions.
do: Address contract breaches or disputes
Terminate unhealthy partner relationships
Renegotiate clearly unfair terms
Engage legal counsel for contract disputes
avoid: Initiating new partnerships at this time
Expecting day-to-day partnership operations to run smoothly

ki.headline: Relationship Maintenance
ki.context_note: Ki day: auspicious for gatherings, not for major deals
ki.detail: Direct social energy toward partner relationship maintenance. Host partner dinners, attend the other company's events, build informal trust channels. Not suitable for major deal negotiations.
do: Host partner relationship maintenance events
Attend the other company's corporate events for networking
Use informal settings to understand the partner's real needs
avoid: Negotiating large contracts or investment deals
Making major partnership direction decisions

sei.headline: Shared Learning Alliance
sei.context_note: Sei day: auspicious for study and accomplishment
sei.detail: Both parties learning and delivering together goes smoothly. Ideal for joint R&D, shared training programs, and knowledge exchange initiatives. Emphasizes mutual growth over short-term profit.
do: Launch joint R&D projects
Build shared training programs
Exchange industry research and market insights
Co-publish white papers or industry reports
avoid: All talk, no action
Ignoring quantification and verification of results

mei.headline: Observation Period
mei.context_note: Mei day: not suitable for major actions
mei.detail: Not the time to launch partnerships, but ideal for evaluating potential partners. Conduct market research, analyze competitors' alliance ecosystems, and inventory your own negotiation leverage.
do: Evaluate potential partners' capabilities and intentions
Analyze the market's partnership ecosystem
Inventory your core competencies and negotiation leverage
avoid: Proactively launching strategic alliance proposals
Making major partnership decisions with insufficient information

gyo.headline: Leverage Accumulation Period
gyo.context_note: Gyo day: actions don't bear fruit
gyo.detail: Everything partnership-related feels like pushing against the wind. Focus on building negotiation leverage: strengthen your product, expand your customer base, build technical moats. Move when you have enough cards to play.
do: Strengthen core capabilities and market position
Accumulate customer case studies and data
Research potential partners' weaknesses and needs
avoid: Launching partnerships with insufficient leverage
Accepting imbalanced partnership terms under pressure

tai.headline: Legacy Partnership Continuation
tai.context_note: Tai day: not suitable for major actions
tai.detail: Best suited for inheriting existing partnership frameworks and developing steadily. Join established industry alliances, take over predecessor's partner relationships, and deepen cooperation within existing agreements.
do: Join existing industry alliances or ecosystems
Inherit and optimize existing partnership relationships
Develop steadily within mature partnership frameworks
avoid: Building entirely new partnerships from scratch
Rushing to change existing partnership terms and direction
